<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The provided `version.py` script is a build helper that generates a version string for the mpv-full-build-git package by querying the git repository state (via `git describe --dirty`) in the source directory. It then substitutes the placeholder `@buildver@` in an input file with this version string and writes the result to an output file, avoiding unnecessary writes if the content hasn't changed.

The script performs no network operations, executes no shell commands beyond a controlled `git describe` call on the local source, and writes only to a designated output file. All operations are confined to version string substitution and are consistent with standard build-time metadata handling in AUR packages. The use of `subprocess.run` with explicit arguments and no shell invocation is secure.

There are no signs of obfuscation, data exfiltration, or unauthorized system modifications. The behavior aligns with typical version stamping practices in VCS-based packages and poses no supply-chain risk.
</details>
